props.command: can I get a custom version of this course?

props.heading: Step one is an email.
props.body: You write to info at humanitarians dot AI, describe the course you want, your audience, and what custom means for your context. They quote a price. No subscription model, no platform fee, no SaaS seat — a quoted price for a deliverable.

props.heading: What ships in the zip: base videos from the canonical course, exam banks tested
props.body: Rights to make it your book, your course, your workshop. Your brand on the front.

props.heading: The zip opens in any CLI — Claude Code, Codex, next year's tool.
props.body: You rewrite examples for your industry, cut the modules your curriculum doesn't cover, add the cases your learners actually deal with. Their content is the starting point; your professional judgment shapes what it becomes.

props.heading: Here's a predict beat.
props.body: What's the one thing that is explicitly not in the zip? Think about what distinguishes a subject-matter expert from an organized set of materials — and what was never theirs to sell in the first place.

props.heading: The answer is judgment.
props.body: Humanitarians AI sells the starting point — vetted videos, tested exams — but what to teach, what to cut, what your specific learners actually need: that was never theirs to sell. The zip makes you faster. It does not make you optional. The teacher stays the teacher.

artifactLines: Email info@humanitarians.ai: the course + your audience → a quoted price. No subscription.
The zip: base videos · exam banks · transform rights — your book, your course, your workshop.
Opens in any CLI — rewrite, rebrand, reorder. No platform, no lock-in.
Phase gate: we ship the base · you transform it · your learners get your version.

props.command: Draft the email to info@humanitarians.ai: name the course I want, describe my audience in two sentences, and ask what a custom version costs and exactly what ships in the zip.
